651	        uses: conda-incubator/setup-miniconda@fc2d68f6413eb2d87b895e92f8584b5b94a10167 # v3.3.0
652	        with:
653	          condarc-file: .github/condarc-${{ matrix.default-channel }}
654	          run-post: false  # skip post cleanup
655	          miniconda-version: ${{ matrix.default-channel == 'defaults' && 'latest' || null }}
656	          miniforge-version: ${{ matrix.default-channel == 'conda-forge' && 'latest' || null }}
657	
658	      - name: Conda Install
659	        run: >
660	          conda install
661	          --yes
662	          --file tests/requirements.txt
663	          --file tests/requirements-ci.txt
664	          --file tests/requirements-s3.txt
665	          python=${{ matrix.python-version }}
666	
667	      - name: Conda Info
668	        # view test env info (not base)
669	        run: python -m conda info --verbose
670	
671	      - name: Conda Config
672	        run: conda config --show-sources
673	
674	      - name: Conda List
675	        run: conda list --show-channel-urls
676	
677	      - name: Setup Shells
678	        # for tests/shell, so only necessary for integration tests
679	        if: matrix.test-type == 'integration'
680	        run: brew update && brew install fish xonsh
681	
682	      - name: Run Tests
683	        run: >
684	          python -m pytest
685	          --cov=conda
686	          --durations-path=durations/${{ runner.os }}.json
687	          --group=${{ matrix.test-group }}
688	          --splits=${{ env.PYTEST_SPLITS }}
689	          -m "${{ env.PYTEST_MARKER }}"
690	
691	      - name: Upload Coverage
692	        uses: codecov/codecov-action@671740ac38dd9b0130fbe1cec585b89eea48d3de # v5.5.2
693	        with:
694	          flags: ${{ runner.os }},${{ runner.arch }},${{ matrix.python-version }},${{ matrix.test-type }}
695	          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}  # required
696	
697	      - name: Upload Test Results
698	        if: '!cancelled()'
699	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@bbbca2ddaa5d8feaa63e36b76fdaad77386f024f # v7.0.0
700	        with:
701	          name: test-results-${{ env.HASH }}
702	          include-hidden-files: true
703	          path: |
704	            .coverage
705	            durations/${{ runner.os }}.json
706	            test-report.xml
707	          retention-days: 1  # temporary, combined in aggregate below
708	
709	  # aggregate and upload
710	  aggregate:
711	    # only aggregate test suite if there are code changes
712	    needs: [changes, windows, linux, linux-benchmarks, linux-qemu, macos]
713	    if: >-
714	      !cancelled()
715	      && (
716	        (!github.event.repository.fork && github.event_name == 'schedule')
717	        || needs.changes.outputs.code == 'true'
718	      )
719	
720	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
721	    steps:
722	      - name: Download Artifacts
723	        uses: actions/download-artifact@70fc10c6e5e1ce46ad2ea6f2b72d43f7d47b13c3 # v8.0.0
724	
725	      - name: Upload Combined Test Results
726	        # provides one downloadable archive of all matrix run test results for further analysis
727	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@bbbca2ddaa5d8feaa63e36b76fdaad77386f024f # v7.0.0
728	        with:
729	          name: test-results-${{ github.sha }}-all
730	          include-hidden-files: true
731	          path: test-results-*
732	          retention-days: 7  # for durations.yml workflow
733	
734	      - name: Test Summary
735	        uses: test-summary/action@31493c76ec9e7aa675f1585d3ed6f1da69269a86 # v2.4
736	        with:
737	          paths: test-results-*/test-report.xml
738	
739	  # required check
740	  analyze:
741	    needs: [windows, linux, linux-qemu, linux-benchmarks, macos, aggregate]
742	    if: '!cancelled()'
743	
744	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
745	    steps:
746	      - name: Determine Success
747	        uses: re-actors/alls-green@05ac9388f0aebcb5727afa17fcccfecd6f8ec5fe # v1.2.2
748	        id: alls-green
749	        with:
750	          # permit jobs to be skipped if there are no code changes (see changes job)
751	          allowed-skips: ${{ toJSON(needs) }}
752	          jobs: ${{ toJSON(needs) }}
753	
754	      - name: Checkout our source
755	        if: always() && github.event_name == 'schedule' && steps.alls-green.outputs.result == 'failure'
756	        uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2
757	
758	      - name: Report failures
759	        if: always() && github.event_name == 'schedule' && steps.alls-green.outputs.result == 'failure'
760	        uses: JasonEtco/create-an-issue@1b14a70e4d8dc185e5cc76d3bec9eab20257b2c5 # v2.9.2
761	        env:
762	          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.AUTO_REPORT_TEST_FAILURE }}
763	          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
764	          TITLE: Tests failed
765	        with:
766	          filename: .github/TEST_FAILURE_REPORT_TEMPLATE.md
767	          update_existing: false
768	
769	  # canary builds
770	  build:
771	    needs: [analyze]
772	    # only build canary build if
773	    # - prior steps succeeded,
774	    # - this is the main repo, and
775	    # - we are on the main, feature, or release branch
776	    if: >-
777	      !cancelled()
778	      && !github.event.repository.fork
779	      && (
780	        github.ref_name == 'main'
781	        || startsWith(github.ref_name, 'feature/')
782	        || endsWith(github.ref_name, '.x')
783	      )
784	    strategy:
785	      matrix:
786	        include:
787	          - runs-on: ubuntu-latest
788	            subdir: linux-64
789	          - runs-on: ubuntu-24.04-arm
790	            subdir: linux-aarch64
791	          - runs-on: macos-latest
792	            subdir: osx-arm64
793	          - runs-on: windows-latest
794	            subdir: win-64
795	    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runs-on }}
796	    steps:
797	      # Clean checkout of specific git ref needed for package metadata version
798	      # which needs env vars GIT_DESCRIBE_TAG and GIT_BUILD_STR:
799	      - name: Checkout Source
800	        uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2
801	        with:
802	          ref: ${{ github.ref }}
803	          clean: true
804	          fetch-depth: 0
805	
806	      # Explicitly use Python 3.11 since each of the OSes has a different default Python
807	      - name: Setup Python
808	        uses: actions/setup-python@a309ff8b426b58ec0e2a45f0f869d46889d02405 # v6.2.0
809	        with:
810	          python-version: '3.11'
811	
812	      - name: Detect Label
813	        shell: python
814	        run: |
815	          import re
816	          from pathlib import Path
817	          from os import environ
818	          from subprocess import check_output
819	
820	          # unless otherwise specified, commits are uploaded to the dev label
821	          # e.g., `main` branch commits
822	          envs = {"ANACONDA_ORG_LABEL": "dev"}
823	
824	          if "${{ github.ref_name }}".startswith("feature/"):
825	              # feature branch commits are uploaded to a custom label
826	              envs["ANACONDA_ORG_LABEL"] = "${{ github.ref_name }}"
827	          elif re.match(r"\d+(\.\d+)+\.x", "${{ github.ref_name }}"):
828	              # release branch commits are added to the rc label
829	              # see https://github.com/conda/infrastructure/issues/760
830	              _, name = "${{ github.repository }}".split("/")
831	              envs["ANACONDA_ORG_LABEL"] = f"rc-{name}-${{ github.ref_name }}"
832	
833	              # if no releases have occurred on this branch yet then `git describe --tag`
834	              # will misleadingly produce a version number relative to the last release
835	              # and not relative to the current release branch, if this is the case we need
836	              # to override the version with a derivative of the branch name
837	
838	              # override the version if `git describe --tag` does not start with the branch version
839	              last_release = check_output(["git", "describe", "--tag"], text=True).strip()
840	              prefix = "${{ github.ref_name }}"[:-1]  # without x suffix
841	              if not last_release.startswith(prefix):
842	                  envs["VERSION_OVERRIDE"] = f"{prefix}0"
843	
844	          Path(environ["GITHUB_ENV"]).write_text("\n".join(f"{name}={value}" for name, value in envs.items()))
845	
846	      - name: Create & Upload
847	        uses: conda/actions/canary-release@7f6830b1428a9bd47f0b068892c77eae95207037 # v26.1.0
848	        env:
849	          # Run conda-build in isolated activation to properly package conda
850	          _CONDA_BUILD_ISOLATED_ACTIVATION: 1
851	        with:
852	          package-name: ${{ github.event.repository.name }}
853	          subdir: ${{ matrix.subdir }}
854	          anaconda-org-channel: conda-canary
855	          anaconda-org-label: ${{ env.ANACONDA_ORG_LABEL }}
856	          anaconda-org-token: ${{ secrets.ANACONDA_ORG_CONDA_CANARY_TOKEN }}
857	
858	  # check conda-build imports
859	  downstream:
860	    # only run test suite if there are code changes
861	    needs: changes
862	    if: needs.changes.outputs.code == 'true'
863	
864	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
865	    defaults:
866	      run:
867	        # https://github.com/conda-incubator/setup-miniconda#use-a-default-shell
868	        shell: bash -el {0}  # bash exit immediately on error + login shell
869	    strategy:
870	      fail-fast: false
871	      matrix:
872	        # check missing imports in one representative environment
873	        python-version: ['3.14']
874	        default-channel: [defaults]
875	
876	    steps:
877	      - name: Checkout conda/conda
878	        uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2
879	        with:
880	          fetch-depth: 0
881	          repository: conda/conda
882	          path: conda
883	
884	      - name: Checkout conda/conda-build
885	        uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2
886	        with:
887	          fetch-depth: 0
888	          repository: conda/conda-build
889	          path: conda-build
890	
891	      - name: Hash + Timestamp + Job
892	        run: echo "HASH=${{ runner.os }}-${{ runner.arch }}-Py${{ matrix.python-version }}-${{ matrix.default-channel }}-${{ github.job }}-$(date -u "+%Y%m")" >> $GITHUB_ENV
893	
894	      - name: Cache Conda
895	        uses: actions/cache@cdf6c1fa76f9f475f3d7449005a359c84ca0f306 # v5.0.3
896	        with:
897	          path: ~/conda_pkgs_dir
898	          key: cache-${{ env.HASH }}
899	
900	      - name: Setup Miniconda
901	        uses: conda-incubator/setup-miniconda@fc2d68f6413eb2d87b895e92f8584b5b94a10167 # v3.3.0
902	        with:
903	          condarc-file: conda/.github/condarc-${{ matrix.default-channel }}
904	          run-post: false  # skip post cleanup
905	
906	      - name: Conda Install
907	        working-directory: conda
908	        run: >
909	          conda install
910	          --yes
911	          --file tests/requirements.txt
912	          --file tests/requirements-${{ runner.os }}.txt
913	          --file tests/requirements-ci.txt
914	          --file tests/requirements-s3.txt
915	          --file ../conda-build/tests/requirements.txt
916	          --file ../conda-build/tests/requirements-ci.txt
917	          --file ../conda-build/tests/requirements-${{ runner.os }}.txt
918	          python=${{ matrix.python-version }}
919	
920	      - name: Install conda, conda-build source code
921	        env:
922	          PIP_NO_BUILD_ISOLATION: '1'
923	        run: |
924	          python -m pip install -e conda -e conda-build -v --no-deps
925	
926	      - name: Conda Info
927	        run: python -m conda info --verbose
928	
929	      - name: Conda Config
930	        run: python -m conda config --show-sources
931	
932	      - name: Conda List
933	        run: python -m conda list --show-channel-urls
934	
935	      - name: Check correct conda, conda-build installed
936	        run: python -c "import conda, conda_build; print(conda.__file__, conda_build.__file__)"
937	
938	      - name: Check conda-build tests for missing imports
939	        working-directory: conda-build
940	        # This test looks for ImportError, not warnings-promoted-to-error. The
941	        # inline script removes the filterwarnings section from pyproject.toml.
942	        # I wasn't able to find a pytest command line argument to do the same.
943	        run: |
944	          python -c "import re, sys, pathlib; pyproject = pathlib.Path('pyproject.toml'); pyproject.write_text(re.sub('(filterwarnings.*?])', '', pyproject.read_text(), flags=re.MULTILINE | re.DOTALL))"
945	          python -m pytest --collect-only

file.content:
name: Tests

on:
  # https://docs.github.com/en/webhooks-and-events/webhooks/webhook-events-and-payloads#push
  push:
    branches:
      - main
      - feature/**
      - '[0-9].*.x'  # e.g., 4.14.x
      - '[0-9][0-9].*.x'  # e.g., 23.3.x

  # https://docs.github.com/en/webhooks-and-events/webhooks/webhook-events-and-payloads#pull_request
  pull_request:

  # https://docs.github.com/en/webhooks-and-events/webhooks/webhook-events-and-payloads#workflow_dispatch
  workflow_dispatch:

  # no payload
  schedule:
      # https://crontab.guru/#37_18_*_*_*
    - cron: 37 18 * * *

concurrency:
  # Concurrency group that uses the workflow name and PR number if available
  # or commit SHA as a fallback. If a new build is triggered under that
  # concurrency group while a previous build is running it will be canceled.
  # Repeated pushes to a PR will cancel all previous builds, while multiple
  # merges to main will not cancel.
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

env:
  # https://conda.github.io/conda-libmamba-solver/user-guide/configuration/#advanced-options
  CONDA_LIBMAMBA_SOLVER_NO_CHANNELS_FROM_INSTALLED: true

jobs:
  # detect whether any code changes are included in this PR
  changes:
    if: github.event_name != 'schedule'

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      # necessary to detect changes
      # https://github.com/dorny/paths-filter#supported-workflows
      pull-requests: read
    outputs:
      code: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.code }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout Source
        uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2
        # dorny/paths-filter needs git clone for non-PR events
        # https://github.com/dorny/paths-filter#supported-workflows
        if: github.event_name != 'pull_request'

      - name: Filter Changes
        uses: dorny/paths-filter@de90cc6fb38fc0963ad72b210f1f284cd68cea36 # v3.0.2
        id: filter
        with:
          filters: |
            code:
              - 'conda/**'
              - 'conda_env/**'
              - 'tests/**'
              - '*.py'
              - 'recipe/**'
              - '.github/workflows/tests.yml'

  # windows test suite
  windows:
    # only run test suite if there are code changes
    needs: changes
    if: >-
      (!github.event.repository.fork && github.event_name == 'schedule')
      || needs.changes.outputs.code == 'true'

    runs-on: windows-latest
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        # test lower version (w/ defaults) and upper version (w/ defaults and conda-forge)
        python-version: ['3.10', '3.14']
        default-channel: [defaults, conda-forge]
        test-type: [unit, integration]
        test-group: [1, 2, 3]
        exclude:
          - default-channel: conda-forge
            python-version: '3.10'
          - test-type: unit
            test-group: 3
    env:
      ErrorActionPreference: Stop  # powershell exit immediately on error
      PYTEST_MARKER: ${{ matrix.test-type == 'unit' && 'not integration' || 'integration' }}
      PYTEST_SPLITS: ${{ matrix.test-type == 'unit' && '2' || '3' }}
      CONDA_TEST_SOLVERS: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && 'libmamba' || 'libmamba,classic' }}

    steps:
      - name: Checkout Source
        uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Set TEMP to D:/Temp
        run: |
          mkdir "D:\\Temp"
          echo "TEMP=D:\\Temp" >> $env:GITHUB_ENV
          echo "TMP=D:\\Temp"  >> $env:GITHUB_ENV

      - name: Hash + Timestamp
        shell: bash  # use bash to run date command
        run: echo "HASH=${{ runner.os }}-${{ runner.arch }}-Py${{ matrix.python-version }}-${{ matrix.default-channel }}-${{ matrix.test-type }}-${{ matrix.test-group }}-$(date -u "+%Y%m")" >> $GITHUB_ENV

      - name: Cache Conda
        uses: actions/cache@cdf6c1fa76f9f475f3d7449005a359c84ca0f306 # v5.0.3
        with:
          # Use faster GNU tar for all runners
          enableCrossOsArchive: true
          path: D:\conda_pkgs_dir
          key: cache-${{ env.HASH }}

      - name: Setup Miniconda
        uses: conda-incubator/setup-miniconda@fc2d68f6413eb2d87b895e92f8584b5b94a10167 # v3.3.0
        with:
          condarc-file: .github\condarc-${{ matrix.default-channel }}
          run-post: false  # skip post cleanup
          pkgs-dirs: D:\conda_pkgs_dir
          installation-dir: D:\conda

      - name: Conda Install
        run: >
          conda install
          --yes
          --file tests\requirements.txt
          --file tests\requirements-${{ runner.os }}.txt
          --file tests\requirements-ci.txt
          --file tests\requirements-s3.txt
          python=${{ matrix.python-version }}

      - name: Conda Info
        # view test env info (not base)
        run: python -m conda info --verbose

      - name: Conda Config
        run: conda config --show-sources

      - name: Conda List
        run: conda list --show-channel-urls

      - name: Setup PowerShell
        # for tests/shell, so only necessary for integration tests
        if: matrix.test-type == 'integration'
        run: |
          $PWSH_STABLE = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\powershell"
          Invoke-Expression "& { $(Invoke-RestMethod https://aka.ms/install-powershell.ps1) } -Destination `"$PWSH_STABLE`""
          $PWSH_PREVIEW = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\powershell-preview"
          Invoke-Expression "& { $(Invoke-RestMethod https://aka.ms/install-powershell.ps1) } -Preview -Destination `"$PWSH_PREVIEW`""
          "PWSHPATH=$PWSH_STABLE;$PWSH_PREVIEW" | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_ENV -Append

      - name: PowerShell Info
        # for tests/shell, so only necessary for integration tests
        if: matrix.test-type == 'integration'
        run: |
          Get-Command -All powershell
          "$env:PWSHPATH" -split ";" | ForEach-Object { Get-Command -All "$_\pwsh" }

      - name: Run Tests
        # Windows is sensitive to long paths, using `--basetemp=${{ runner.temp }} to
        # keep the test directories shorter
        run: >
          python -m pytest
          --cov=conda
          --basetemp=${{ runner.temp }}
          --durations-path=durations\${{ runner.os }}.json
          --group=${{ matrix.test-group }}
          --splits=${{ env.PYTEST_SPLITS }}
          -m "${{ env.PYTEST_MARKER }}"

      - name: Upload Coverage
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@671740ac38dd9b0130fbe1cec585b89eea48d3de # v5.5.2
        with:
          flags: ${{ runner.os }},${{ runner.arch }},${{ matrix.python-version }},${{ matrix.test-type }}
          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}  # required

      - name: Upload Test Results
        if: '!cancelled()'
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@bbbca2ddaa5d8feaa63e36b76fdaad77386f024f # v7.0.0
        with:
          name: test-results-${{ env.HASH }}
          include-hidden-files: true
          path: |
            .coverage
            durations\${{ runner.os }}.json
            test-report.xml
          retention-days: 1  # temporary, combined in aggregate below

  # linux test suite
  linux:
    # only run test suite if there are code changes
    needs: changes
    if: >-
      (!github.event.repository.fork && github.event_name == 'schedule')
      || needs.changes.outputs.code == 'true'

    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runs-on }}
    defaults:
      run:
        # https://github.com/conda-incubator/setup-miniconda#use-a-default-shell
        shell: bash -el {0}  # bash exit immediately on error + login shell
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        # test all lower versions (w/ defaults) and upper version (w/ defaults and conda-forge)
        runs-on:
          - ubuntu-latest  # linux-64
          - ubuntu-24.04-arm  # linux-aarch64
        python-version: ['3.10', '3.11', '3.12', '3.13', '3.14']
        default-channel: [defaults, conda-forge]
        test-type: [unit, integration]
        test-group: [1, 2, 3]
        exclude:
          - python-version: '3.10'
            default-channel: conda-forge
          - python-version: '3.11'
            default-channel: conda-forge
          - python-version: '3.12'
            default-channel: conda-forge
          - test-type: unit
            test-group: 3
          - runs-on: ubuntu-24.04-arm
            python-version: '3.10'
          - runs-on: ubuntu-24.04-arm
            python-version: '3.11'
          - runs-on: ubuntu-24.04-arm
            python-version: '3.12'
    env:
      PYTEST_MARKER: ${{ matrix.test-type == 'unit' && 'not integration' || 'integration' }}
      PYTEST_SPLITS: ${{ matrix.test-type == 'unit' && '2' || '3' }}
      CONDA_TEST_SOLVERS: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && ( matrix.python-version != '3.14' || matrix.default-channel == 'conda-forge' ) && 'libmamba' || 'libmamba,classic' }}

    steps:
      - name: Checkout Source
        uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Hash + Timestamp
        run: echo "HASH=${{ runner.os }}-${{ runner.arch }}-Py${{ matrix.python-version }}-${{ matrix.default-channel }}-${{ matrix.test-type }}-${{ matrix.test-group }}-$(date -u "+%Y%m")" >> $GITHUB_ENV

      - name: Cache Conda
        uses: actions/cache@cdf6c1fa76f9f475f3d7449005a359c84ca0f306 # v5.0.3
        with:
          path: ~/conda_pkgs_dir
          key: cache-${{ env.HASH }}

      - name: Setup Miniconda
        uses: conda-incubator/setup-miniconda@fc2d68f6413eb2d87b895e92f8584b5b94a10167 # v3.3.0
        with:
          condarc-file: .github/condarc-${{ matrix.default-channel }}
          run-post: false  # skip post cleanup
          miniconda-version: ${{ matrix.default-channel == 'defaults' && 'latest' || null }}
          miniforge-version: ${{ matrix.default-channel == 'conda-forge' && 'latest' || null }}

      - name: Conda Install
        run: >
          conda install
          --yes
          --file tests/requirements.txt
          --file tests/requirements-${{ runner.os }}.txt
          --file tests/requirements-ci.txt
          --file tests/requirements-s3.txt
          python=${{ matrix.python-version }}

      - name: Conda Info

          ". /opt/conda/etc/profile.d/conda.sh

          --file tests/requirements.txt
          --file tests/requirements-${{ runner.os }}.txt

  # aggregate and upload

    needs: [changes, windows, linux, linux-benchmarks, linux-qemu, macos]
    if: >-
      !cancelled()
      && (
        (!github.event.repository.fork && github.event_name == 'schedule')
        || needs.changes.outputs.code == 'true'
      )

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Download Artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@70fc10c6e5e1ce46ad2ea6f2b72d43f7d47b13c3 # v8.0.0

      - name: Upload Combined Test Results
        # provides one downloadable archive of all matrix run test results for further analysis
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@bbbca2ddaa5d8feaa63e36b76fdaad77386f024f # v7.0.0
        with:
          name: test-results-${{ github.sha }}-all
          include-hidden-files: true
          path: test-results-*
          retention-days: 7  # for durations.yml workflow

      - name: Test Summary
        uses: test-summary/action@31493c76ec9e7aa675f1585d3ed6f1da69269a86 # v2.4
        with:
          paths: test-results-*/test-report.xml

  # required check
  analyze:
    needs: [windows, linux, linux-qemu, linux-benchmarks, macos, aggregate]
    if: '!cancelled()'

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Determine Success
        uses: re-actors/alls-green@05ac9388f0aebcb5727afa17fcccfecd6f8ec5fe # v1.2.2
        id: alls-green
        with:
          # permit jobs to be skipped if there are no code changes (see changes job)
          allowed-skips: ${{ toJSON(needs) }}
          jobs: ${{ toJSON(needs) }}

      - name: Checkout our source
        if: always() && github.event_name == 'schedule' && steps.alls-green.outputs.result == 'failure'
        uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2

      - name: Report failures
        if: always() && github.event_name == 'schedule' && steps.alls-green.outputs.result == 'failure'
        uses: JasonEtco/create-an-issue@1b14a70e4d8dc185e5cc76d3bec9eab20257b2c5 # v2.9.2
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.AUTO_REPORT_TEST_FAILURE }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          TITLE: Tests failed
        with:
          filename: .github/TEST_FAILURE_REPORT_TEMPLATE.md
          update_existing: false

  # canary builds
  build:
    needs: [analyze]
    # only build canary build if
    # - prior steps succeeded,
    # - this is the main repo, and
    # - we are on the main, feature, or release branch
    if: >-
      !cancelled()
      && !github.event.repository.fork
      && (
        github.ref_name == 'main'
        || startsWith(github.ref_name, 'feature/')
        || endsWith(github.ref_name, '.x')
      )
    strategy:
      matrix:
        include:
          - runs-on: ubuntu-latest
            subdir: linux-64
          - runs-on: ubuntu-24.04-arm
            subdir: linux-aarch64
          - runs-on: macos-latest
            subdir: osx-arm64
          - runs-on: windows-latest
            subdir: win-64
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runs-on }}
    steps:
      # Clean checkout of specific git ref needed for package metadata version
      # which needs env vars GIT_DESCRIBE_TAG and GIT_BUILD_STR:
      - name: Checkout Source
        uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.ref }}
          clean: true
          fetch-depth: 0

      # Explicitly use Python 3.11 since each of the OSes has a different default Python
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@a309ff8b426b58ec0e2a45f0f869d46889d02405 # v6.2.0
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'

      - name: Detect Label
        shell: python
        run: |
          import re
          from pathlib import Path
          from os import environ
          from subprocess import check_output

          # unless otherwise specified, commits are uploaded to the dev label
          # e.g., `main` branch commits
          envs = {"ANACONDA_ORG_LABEL": "dev"}

          if "${{ github.ref_name }}".startswith("feature/"):
              # feature branch commits are uploaded to a custom label
              envs["ANACONDA_ORG_LABEL"] = "${{ github.ref_name }}"
          elif re.match(r"\d+(\.\d+)+\.x", "${{ github.ref_name }}"):
              # release branch commits are added to the rc label
              # see https://github.com/conda/infrastructure/issues/760
              _, name = "${{ github.repository }}".split("/")
              envs["ANACONDA_ORG_LABEL"] = f"rc-{name}-${{ github.ref_name }}"

              # if no releases have occurred on this branch yet then `git describe --tag`
              # will misleadingly produce a version number relative to the last release
              # and not relative to the current release branch, if this is the case we need
              # to override the version with a derivative of the branch name

              # override the version if `git describe --tag` does not start with the branch version
              last_release = check_output(["git", "describe", "--tag"], text=True).strip()
              prefix = "${{ github.ref_name }}"[:-1]  # without x suffix
              if not last_release.startswith(prefix):
                  envs["VERSION_OVERRIDE"] = f"{prefix}0"

          Path(environ["GITHUB_ENV"]).write_text("\n".join(f"{name}={value}" for name, value in envs.items()))

      - name: Create & Upload
        uses: conda/actions/canary-release@7f6830b1428a9bd47f0b068892c77eae95207037 # v26.1.0
        env:
          # Run conda-build in isolated activation to properly package conda
          _CONDA_BUILD_ISOLATED_ACTIVATION: 1
        with:
          package-name: ${{ github.event.repository.name }}
          subdir: ${{ matrix.subdir }}
          anaconda-org-channel: conda-canary
          anaconda-org-label: ${{ env.ANACONDA_ORG_LABEL }}
          anaconda-org-token: ${{ secrets.ANACONDA_ORG_CONDA_CANARY_TOKEN }}

  # check conda-build imports
  downstream:
    # only run test suite if there are code changes
    needs: changes
    if: needs.changes.outputs.code == 'true'

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        # https://github.com/conda-incubator/setup-miniconda#use-a-default-shell
        shell: bash -el {0}  # bash exit immediately on error + login shell
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        # check missing imports in one representative environment
        python-version: ['3.14']
        default-channel: [defaults]

    steps:
      - name: Checkout conda/conda
        uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          repository: conda/conda
          path: conda

      - name: Checkout conda/conda-build
        uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          repository: conda/conda-build
          path: conda-build

      - name: Hash + Timestamp + Job
        run: echo "HASH=${{ runner.os }}-${{ runner.arch }}-Py${{ matrix.python-version }}-${{ matrix.default-channel }}-${{ github.job }}-$(date -u "+%Y%m")" >> $GITHUB_ENV

      - name: Cache Conda
        uses: actions/cache@cdf6c1fa76f9f475f3d7449005a359c84ca0f306 # v5.0.3
        with:
          path: ~/conda_pkgs_dir
          key: cache-${{ env.HASH }}

      - name: Setup Miniconda
        uses: conda-incubator/setup-miniconda@fc2d68f6413eb2d87b895e92f8584b5b94a10167 # v3.3.0
        with:
          condarc-file: conda/.github/condarc-${{ matrix.default-channel }}
          run-post: false  # skip post cleanup

      - name: Conda Install
        working-directory: conda
        run: >
          conda install
          --yes
          --file tests/requirements.txt
          --file tests/requirements-${{ runner.os }}.txt
          --file tests/requirements-ci.txt
          --file tests/requirements-s3.txt
          --file ../conda-build/tests/requirements.txt
          --file ../conda-build/tests/requirements-ci.txt
          --file ../conda-build/tests/requirements-${{ runner.os }}.txt
          python=${{ matrix.python-version }}

      - name: Install conda, conda-build source code
        env:
          PIP_NO_BUILD_ISOLATION: '1'
        run: |
          python -m pip install -e conda -e conda-build -v --no-deps

      - name: Conda Info
        run: python -m conda info --verbose

      - name: Conda Config
        run: python -m conda config --show-sources

      - name: Conda List
        run: python -m conda list --show-channel-urls

      - name: Check correct conda, conda-build installed
        run: python -c "import conda, conda_build; print(conda.__file__, conda_build.__file__)"

      - name: Check conda-build tests for missing imports
        working-directory: conda-build
        # This test looks for ImportError, not warnings-promoted-to-error. The
        # inline script removes the filterwarnings section from pyproject.toml.
        # I wasn't able to find a pytest command line argument to do the same.

FROM ubuntu:22.04

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV CONDA_LIBMAMBA_SOLVER_NO_CHANNELS_FROM_INSTALLED=true

# Install system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    curl \
    wget \
    git \
    ca-certificates \
    bzip2 \
    libffi-dev \
    libssl-dev \
    zlib1g-dev \
    bash \
    ash \
    csh \
    fish \
    tcsh \
    zsh \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Miniconda
RUN curl -fsSL https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-latest-Linux-x86_64.sh -o /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
    bash /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p /opt/conda && \
    rm /tmp/miniconda.sh

ENV PATH=/opt/conda/bin:$PATH

# Create the test environment directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy the repository
COPY repo/ /app/

# Create condarc file for defaults channel
RUN mkdir -p /root && \
    echo "channels:" > /root/.condarc && \
    echo "  - defaults" >> /root/.condarc && \
    echo "anaconda_anon_usage: false" >> /root/.condarc

# Install conda dependencies
RUN conda install -y \
    --file tests/requirements.txt \
    --file tests/requirements-Linux.txt \
    --file tests/requirements-ci.txt \
    --file tests/requirements-s3.txt \
    python=3.11 && \
    conda clean -afy

# Initialize all shells for integration tests
RUN /opt/conda/bin/python -m conda init --all

# Copy and set up run.sh
COPY run.sh /run.sh
RUN chmod +x /run.sh

ENTRYPOINT ["/run.sh"]

FROM ubuntu:22.04

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV CONDA_LIBMAMBA_SOLVER_NO_CHANNELS_FROM_INSTALLED=true

# Install system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    curl \
    wget \
    git \
    ca-certificates \
    bzip2 \
    libffi-dev \
    libssl-dev \
    zlib1g-dev \
    bash \
    ash \
    csh \
    fish \
    tcsh \
    zsh \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Miniconda
RUN curl -fsSL https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-latest-Linux-x86_64.sh -o /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
    bash /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p /opt/conda && \
    rm /tmp/miniconda.sh

ENV PATH=/opt/conda/bin:$PATH

# Create the test environment directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy the repository
COPY repo/ /app/

# Create condarc file for defaults channel
RUN mkdir -p /root && \
    echo "channels:" > /root/.condarc && \
    echo "  - defaults" >> /root/.condarc && \
    echo "anaconda_anon_usage: false" >> /root/.condarc

# Install conda dependencies
RUN conda install -y \
    --channel defaults \
    --file tests/requirements.txt \
    --file tests/requirements-Linux.txt \
    --file tests/requirements-ci.txt \
    --file tests/requirements-s3.txt \
    python=3.11 && \
    conda clean -afy

# Initialize all shells for integration tests
RUN /opt/conda/bin/python -m conda init --all

# Copy and set up run.sh
COPY run.sh /run.sh
RUN chmod +x /run.sh

ENTRYPOINT ["/run.sh"]

FROM ubuntu:22.04

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV CONDA_LIBMAMBA_SOLVER_NO_CHANNELS_FROM_INSTALLED=true

# Install system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    curl \
    wget \
    git \
    ca-certificates \
    bzip2 \
    libffi-dev \
    libssl-dev \
    zlib1g-dev \
    bash \
    ash \
    csh \
    fish \
    tcsh \
    zsh \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Miniconda
RUN curl -fsSL https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-latest-Linux-x86_64.sh -o /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
    bash /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p /opt/conda && \
    rm /tmp/miniconda.sh

ENV PATH=/opt/conda/bin:$PATH
ENV CONDA_OVERRIDE_CHANNELS_TOS=yes

# Create the test environment directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy the repository
COPY repo/ /app/

# Create condarc file for defaults channel
RUN mkdir -p /root && \
    echo "channels:" > /root/.condarc && \
    echo "  - defaults" >> /root/.condarc && \
    echo "anaconda_anon_usage: false" >> /root/.condarc

# Accept conda TOS for defaults channel
RUN conda tos accept --override-channels --channel https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/main || true

# Install conda dependencies
RUN conda install -y \
    --channel defaults \
    --file tests/requirements.txt \
    --file tests/requirements-Linux.txt \
    --file tests/requirements-ci.txt \
    --file tests/requirements-s3.txt \
    python=3.11 && \
    conda clean -afy

# Initialize all shells for integration tests
RUN /opt/conda/bin/python -m conda init --all

# Copy and set up run.sh
COPY run.sh /run.sh
RUN chmod +x /run.sh

ENTRYPOINT ["/run.sh"]
